input.background: A study was carried out to see if patients whose skin did not respond to dinitrochlorobenzene (DNCB), a contact allergen, would show an equally negative response to croton oil, a skin irritant. The study also examined whether DNCB reactivity is related to the stage of skin cancer in these patients.
input.question: The authors reported 'no correlation' between the two tests. Carry out an analysis appropriate to the clinical question posed.

output.answer: It is essential to use an analysis appropriate for paired data. The proportions negative to croton oil and DNCB were 44 / 173 = 25.4% and 69 / 173 = 39.9% respectively. A 95% CI for the difference between these proportions, using the method given in section 10.4, is from 5% to 24%. The hypothesis test that the proportions of patients negative to the two skin tests are the same is evaluated by calculating z = (48 - 23) / sqrt(48 + 23) = 2.97, which, from Table B2, corresponds to P = 0.003. There is thus strong evidence that fewer of these patients show negative reactions to croton oil than DNCB.
